name: data-engineering-data-pipeline
description: You are a data pipeline architecture expert specializing in scalable, reliable, and cost-effective data pipelines for batch and streaming data processing.
qualityReason: Clear focus on pipeline best practices
capabilities: Design ETL/ELT, Lambda, Kappa, and Lakehouse architectures based on requirements
Implement batch and streaming ingestion with watermark columns and retry logic
Create Airflow and Prefect orchestration workflows with task dependencies
Build dbt staging and marts layers with schema and data quality tests
Configure Delta Lake and Apache Iceberg storage with ACID transactions
Set up monitoring dashboards and alerts using CloudWatch, Prometheus, and Grafana
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static analyzer findings are false positives. The skill is documentation-only, providing architectural guidance and educational code examples. No executable code, external commands, or security risks detected. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Designing production-ready data pipelines is complex and error-prone. This skill provides proven architectural patterns and implementation guidance for ETL, streaming, and lakehouse systems.
limitations: Provides architectural guidance only - does not execute pipelines or connect to live systems
Example connection strings and configurations require adaptation for production environments
Does not replace domain-specific compliance or governance requirements
Cost estimates are approximate and vary by cloud provider and data volume

useCases.title: Greenfield Pipeline Architecture
useCases.description: Design a complete data pipeline from scratch for a startup migrating from spreadsheets to a modern data stack.

useCases.title: Streaming Migration Strategy
useCases.description: Convert existing batch pipelines to real-time streaming architecture using Kafka and stream processing frameworks.

useCases.title: Data Quality Framework Implementation
useCases.description: Implement comprehensive data quality checks using Great Expectations and dbt tests with automated alerting.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Pipeline Design
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to build a data pipeline that extracts data from PostgreSQL daily, transforms it, and loads it to a data warehouse. What architecture should I use and what are the key components?
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner asking for basic ETL guidance

promptTemplates.title: Streaming Architecture Selection
promptTemplates.prompt: We have high-volume event data from our application and need near-real-time analytics. Compare Lambda vs Kappa architecture for our use case with 1M events per minute.

promptTemplates.title: Data Quality Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to implement data quality checks for our orders table using Great Expectations. We need to validate uniqueness of order IDs, non-null customer IDs, and positive order amounts.

promptTemplates.title: Cost Optimization Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Our monthly data pipeline costs have doubled. Review our architecture and provide specific recommendations to reduce costs while maintaining SLA. Current stack: Airflow, Spark, S3, Redshift.

bestPractices: Assess data sources, volume, latency requirements, and target systems before selecting architecture patterns
Implement incremental processing with watermark columns to avoid reprocessing entire datasets
Add data quality gates at each pipeline stage with automated alerts on validation failures
antiPatterns: Copying production patterns without adapting to specific data volume and velocity requirements
Choosing architecture based on trends rather than business needs and team capabilities
Prioritizing features over monitoring, observability, and operational runbooks

faq.question: Should I use Lambda or Kappa architecture for real-time analytics?
faq.answer: Choose Lambda when you need both batch accuracy and low-latency views with complex aggregations. Choose Kappa for simpler stream-only processing where replay capability is sufficient. Kappa reduces operational complexity but requires robust stream processing infrastructure.

faq.question: How do I handle late-arriving data in streaming pipelines?
faq.answer: Use event time processing with watermarks to define lateness thresholds. Implement side outputs for late data that can be reprocessed. For critical data, maintain a batch correction job that runs periodically to fix any missed records.

faq.question: What file format should I use for data lake storage?
faq.answer: Use Parquet for columnar analytics workloads with compression and predicate pushdown. Delta Lake or Iceberg add ACID transactions, schema evolution, and time travel on top of Parquet. Choose based on your need for transactions and metadata management.

faq.question: When should I use dbt versus Spark for transformations?
faq.answer: Use dbt for SQL-based transformations in your data warehouse with built-in testing and documentation. Use Spark for large-scale data processing, complex transformations requiring Python/Scala, or when working with data lakes before loading to warehouse.

faq.question: How do I achieve exactly-once processing in streaming?
faq.answer: Combine idempotent sinks with transactional processing. Use Kafka transactions for atomic writes, checkpoint state for recovery, and design idempotent operations. For databases, use upsert operations with unique constraints to prevent duplicates.

faq.question: What monitoring metrics are essential for data pipelines?
faq.answer: Track: records processed and failed per stage, end-to-end latency, data freshness, pipeline success rate, and resource utilization. Set alerts on SLA breaches, error rate spikes, and data quality failures. Monitor trends to identify capacity issues before they cause outages.
